subject: Re: Two Things
body:
refuse

----- Original Message ----
From: "DKIESQ@aol.com" <DKIESQ@aol.com>
To: jeeproject@yahoo.com
Sent: Friday, October 13, 2006 2:08:05 PM
Subject: Two Things



 

I.    Non-Disclosure Agreement - After e-mailing you 
the message I got from Larry and the request that you confirm whether or not you 
want a non-disclosure document to be sent to Lufthanza in order to obtain info 
regarding the BBJ, I spoke directly with Sean, who clarified the 
following:

 

1.    He does not know why Lufthanza is asking for the 
NDA

 

2.    Sean has never before been asked by a seller to 
get an NDA from a prospective Buyer before information regarding the aircraft is 
delivered to the prospective buyer, except when the manufacturer or 
completion center was issuing a special proposal to the prospective buyer, in 
which case the manufacturer or completion center was trying to keep their 
pricing confidential (according to Sean).  Upon reflection, Sean guessed 
that this may be the reason that Lufthanza, who put together the completion 
package, requested the NDA.

 

3.    In any event, when I specifically asked Sean 
if Lufthanza would deliver the info to you regarding the BBJ without the 
NDA, Sean advised me that Lufthanza would not deliver the 
information. 

 

Please advise whether you wish to provide the Non-Disclosure 
Agreement

 

II.    Conflict Waiver - Susan Cohen who is a labor 
lawyer at Vorys Sater called me to ask for your view and permission for Susan 
and Vorys to accept engagement by the Village of New Albany for 
representation in connection with Economic Development issues.  Susan 
cannot be more specific because there has not been any specific task or question 
assigned to Vorys Sater relating to economic development issues; however, Susan 
recalls that there have been times in the past, and Susan can think of 
situations in the future, when NACO has been and will again be in a somewhat 
adverse position to the Village of New Albany, so Susan will not accept the 
engagement without a formal waiver from both NACO and the Village of New Albany, 
in which the village would acknowledge Vory's right to represent NACO in any 
adversarial proceedings, presumably, other than proceedings adversarial to the 
Village of New Albany involving the subject matter of Vory's representation of 
the Village of New Albany.

 

My inclination is not to allow it; who knows what, when and how many 
conflicts may arise.

 

Do I have your permission to tell Susan to refuse representation of 
the Village of New Albnay.

 

Darren K. 
Indyke
Attorney-At-Law
457 Madison Avenue
4th Floor
New York, New 
York 10022
Tel: 212-750-1176
Fax: 212-750-0381
e-mail: dkiesq@aol.com
 
 
Confidentiality 
Notice: This communication and any attachments contain information from Darren 
K. Indyke, Esq. that constitute attorney work product or that are otherwise 
confidential and/or privileged. Except for personal use by the intended 
recipient, or as expressly authorized by the sender, any person who receives 
this information is prohibited from disclosing, copying, distributing, and/or 
using it. If you have received this communication in error, please immediately 
delete it and all copies, and promptly notify the sender at the above telephone 
number or electronic mail address. Nothing in this communication is intended to 
operate as an electronic signature under applicable 
law.

refuse

----- Original Message ----
From: "DKIESQ@aol.com" <DKIESQ@aol.com>
To: jeeproject@yahoo.com
Sent: Friday, October 13, 2006 2:08:05 PM
Subject: Two Things

I.    Non-Disclosure Agreement - After e-mailing you the message I got from Larry and the request that you confirm whether or not you want a non-disclosure document to be sent to Lufthanza in order to obtain info regarding the BBJ, I spoke directly with Sean, who clarified the following:
 
1.    He does not know why Lufthanza is asking for the NDA
 
2.    Sean has never before been asked by a seller to get an NDA from a prospective Buyer before information regarding the aircraft is delivered to the prospective buyer, except when the manufacturer or completion center was issuing a special proposal to the prospective buyer, in which case the manufacturer or completion center was trying to keep their pricing confidential (according to Sean).  Upon reflection, Sean guessed that this may be the reason that Lufthanza, who put together the completion package, requested the NDA.
 
3.    In any event, when I specifically asked Sean if Lufthanza would deliver the info to you regarding the BBJ without the NDA, Sean advised me that Lufthanza would not deliver the information. 
 
Please advise whether you wish to provide the Non-Disclosure Agreement
 
II.    Conflict Waiver - Susan Cohen who is a labor lawyer at Vorys Sater called me to ask for your view and permission for Susan and Vorys to accept engagement by the Village of New Albany for representation in connection with Economic Development issues.  Susan cannot be more specific because there has not been any specific task or question assigned to Vorys Sater relating to economic development issues; however, Susan recalls that there have been times in the past, and Susan can think of situations in the future, when NACO has been and will again be in a somewhat adverse position to the Village of New Albany, so Susan will not accept the engagement without a formal waiver from both NACO and the Village of New Albany, in which the village would acknowledge Vory's right to represent NACO in any adversarial proceedings, presumably, other than proceedings adversarial to the Village of New Albany involving the subject matter of Vory's representation of the Village of New Albany.
 
My inclination is not to allow it; who knows what, when and how many conflicts may arise.
 
Do I have your permission to tell Susan to refuse representation of the Village of New Albnay.
 
Darren K. Indyke
